title: Blank passphrase not allowed on signing key
body:
When trying to sync or copy images with `--sign-by-sigstore-private-key` using a key that doesn't have a passphrase I get the error

```
FATA[0002] Error copying ref "docker://ghcr.io/siderolabs/glibc:2.41": private key passphrase not provided
```

The same key works with cosign via
```
cosign sign -y --key cosign.key $IMAGE
```

If my key has a passphrase the error is not shown and copy continues.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report!

For various bad reasons, Go programs tend to be sloppy about the difference between “empty” and “missing”.

(There is no such thing as “a [cosign] key that doesn’t have a passphrase”; encryption is mandatory in this private key file format, it’s just that the passphrase is an empty string.)

https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2720 should fix it; in the meantime, as a workaround, you can use `--sign-by-sigstore` with a passphrase file option pointing at an empty file.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
